justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Scheduling lag metric gap identified as primary SLO gap — called out as CRITICAL with explicit mention of scheduled_time minus processed_time. (2) Retry tracking gap identified — retry_attempts_total and retry_queue_depth both flagged as CRITICAL missing metrics for the 24h retry SLO. (3) Current alert critiqued as symptom-based — 'current alerting is purely error-count based and will not catch latency degradation', 'catches only 1 of 6 identified failure modes'. (4) Specific metric recommendations provided — renewal_scheduling_lag_seconds histogram, retry_attempts_total counter, renewals_pending/renewals_overdue gauges, renewal_slo_compliance ratio. (5) SLO-based alerting recommended — burn-rate alert on p95 scheduling lag and SLO compliance ratio dashboard. (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough — identified all critical gaps and went well beyond the rubric with additional findings (health check insufficiency, missing correlation IDs, duplicate renewal risk, circuit breaker absence)
Strong framing of the core problem: 'two explicit SLOs but ZERO metrics that measure either SLO' — immediately communicates the severity
Excellent operational empathy — the '3am on-call engineer' framing concretely illustrates why the monitoring gaps matter

Specific, actionable metric names recommended (renewal_scheduling_lag_seconds, retry_attempts_total, renewals_pending, renewals_overdue, duplicate_renewal_attempts_total) rather than generic 'add more metrics'
Correctly identified the health check as creating a false sense of reliability — orchestration platforms would report healthy while scheduler is stalled
Burn-rate alerting recommendation with concrete threshold (p95 > 30min) shows SLO-engineering understanding

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Scheduling lag metric gap identified as primary SLO-monitoring gap — called out as 'CRITICAL' and 'the single most important missing metric' with specific recommendation for renewal_scheduling_lag_seconds histogram. (2) Retry tracking gap identified — retry pipeline called 'completely invisible' with specific metrics recommended (renewal_retry_attempts_total, renewal_retry_queue_depth). (3) Current alert critiqued as symptom-based — explicitly noted that the 10 errors/5min alert 'treats all errors equally' and will 'either over-alert on declines or under-alert on outages', distinguishing it from SLO-based alerting. (4) Specific metric recommendations provided: scheduling lag histogram, retry_attempts_total counter, retry_queue_depth gauge, renewal_pending_total gauge. (5) SLO-based alerting recommended — multi-severity alert tiers at 50% and 80% SLO budget, burn-rate style alerting on p99 exceeding 30 minutes. (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough analysis — identified not just the required gaps but also silent data loss, health endpoint false confidence, and reconciliation needs
Contract compliance section directly states both SLOs have 'zero real-time monitoring' — cuts straight to the core issue
The 3am on-call framing is excellent — concretely demonstrates why current monitoring fails in practice
Failure modes section covers 6 distinct scenarios with specific, actionable recommendations for each
Correctly identifies that logs contain the data (scheduled_time, processed_time) but it's not exposed as alertable metrics — a nuanced observation
Escalations are well-prioritized with clear routing and reasoning
Distinguishes transient vs permanent billing errors as a monitoring gap — goes beyond the rubric requirements

trials.justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Scheduling lag metric gap identified as primary SLO-monitoring gap — called out as escalation #1 and repeatedly emphasized throughout. (2) Retry tracking gap identified with specific metrics (retry_queue_depth, retry_attempts_total, retry_lag_seconds) — escalation #2. (3) Current alert explicitly critiqued as symptom-based: 'billing_api_errors_total > 10 in 5min' fires on errors not lateness, called out in failure mode #4 and contract_compliance. (4) Specific metric recommendations: renewal_scheduling_lag_seconds histogram, renewal_retry_attempts_total counter, plus 5 additional missing metrics. (5) SLO-based alerting recommended: renewal_slo_compliance_ratio gauge over rolling window, p95 lag alerting with burn-rate-style buffer (alert at 30min for 60min SLO). (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough — identified 7 missing metrics with precise names, labels, and types
Strong articulation of WHY the current alert is insufficient: fires on errors not lateness, fires on symptoms not SLO compliance
Identified silent scheduler failure as an undetectable failure mode — goes beyond the rubric requirements
Practical on-call perspective: 'engineer at 3am cannot answer these questions' framing demonstrates operational maturity
Correctly identified absence-of-work detection gap — a sophisticated monitoring concern
Good confidence calibration — acknowledges uncertainty about retry internals and timeout config while being confident on the clear gaps
